description:
Letter from the amīr Kanz al-Dawla, probably in Aswān, to al-Bazīl, the deputy (nāʾib) of the eparch Abū l-Khayr Darmā, in Qalʿat Ibrīm. The sender is likely the same as in docs. 1 and 8 (see Khan, Medieval Nubia, p. 52). The sender has the epithet al-ʿĀḍidī, indicating that he was affiliated with the caliph al-ʿĀḍid (r. 1160–71). The eparch Darmā (ⲇⲁⲣⲙⲉ) is mentioned in Nubian documents dating from 1155 to 1187; he appears also in doc. 23. The current letter is sent with a man called "the head" (al-raʾīs) and the addressee is asked to protect him from harassment in Nubia. The fragment was reused for a private letter (doc. 35, PGPID 40707). 

Alternative readings (ASE): 
In l. 5 it is possible to read ومن معه من تجار وغيرهم ("and those merchants and others who are with him")

Khan, Qaṣr Ibrīm, doc. 26.
description_he: מכתב מהאמיר כַּנְז אַל-דַּוְלָה, ככל הנראה מאַסְוָאן, אל אַל-בַּזִיל, סגן הנסיך (אֶפַּרְכּ) אַבּוּ אַל-ח'ַיְר דַּרְמָא, בקצר אִבְרִים; השולח נושא את הכינוי אל-עאד'ידי, המצביע על זיקה לח'ליף אל-עאד'יד (1160–1171 לספירה); הנמען מתבקש להגן על האיש המכונה 'הראש' (אל-ראיס) מהטרדות בנוביה; השבר שומש מחדש כמכתב פרטי (PGPID 40707)
